Pariprashnena — Q&A Archive

A read-only archive of 1,235 questions and 14,977 answers from a Gauḍīya Vaiṣṇava forum (2007–2012).

Does anyone know where I can find the Vaisnava Primer teachers edition?

Philosophy · asked by user [] · 2007-10-09 · 5 answers
This is an excellent syllabus that was made in about 1987 New Govardhana Press (San Diego i think). The teachers edition comes with a section in the back that has questions and outlines. This was so well done that Im surprised its not in print everywhere.

Does anyone know what Im describing here? If so, how can I get a copy? (Ill pay for printing, postage and a donation for your troubles)
user [24] · 2007-10-09
a digital version would be great. I can just get it printed here.
user [2] · 2007-10-09
I based the Vedalearn Bhakta Program http://vedalearn.wordpress.com on it, but neither couldnt find the teachers syllabus. I modified certain parts not relevant nowadays and made the questions test part.
See http://vedalearn.wordpress.com/2006/11/28/curriculum/ for the Curriculum.
Test Zone http://vedalearn.wordpress.com/online-tests/
Here http://vedalearn.wordpress.com/read-download/ you can download the material in pdf.
user [24] · 2007-10-10
excellent. too bad we cant find the teachers edition. but Id be interested in getting on the test questions here:

http://vedalearn.wordpress.com/online-tests/

could I use these with the course were doing here?
user [24] · 2007-10-10
looks like ill need a password to access the questions? is that a possibility?
user [2] · 2007-10-10
I just emailed you the password for the test zone.
Vedalearn was conceived as a Bhakta Program online, feel free to direct your people to the site, answer their questions in the site provided zone and I can email you test results etc, if they are under your care.
You can print the material and use it as you please, that is on my side, now the original Vaishnava Primer is copyrighted by BBT, I have the written permission to use it online. Not sure about offline use on the part of the BBT.
Oh, I do not have a hard copy of the tests material, if you could do one, will be a nice contribution for offline testing. If you happen to do the work, please email me the pdfs.
